name: Vultr
what_it_does: Affordable cloud provider offering VPS, bare metal, managed Kubernetes, and GPU instances across 32+ global locations with a clean REST API for programmatic infrastructure management.
use_cases: Deploying low-cost VPS instances for agent compute workloads globally
Provisioning managed Kubernetes clusters (VKE) via API for container workloads
Spinning up GPU instances for ML inference without enterprise cloud pricing
Automating server lifecycle in CI/CD and agent-driven infrastructure pipelines
Managing block storage, object storage, and load balancers via API
not_for: Workloads requiring deep AWS/GCP managed service integration
Enterprise compliance requirements (limited certifications vs hyperscalers)
Primary EU-regulated workloads (Hetzner has stronger EU data sovereignty)
Organizations needing advanced networking features like Transit Gateway
best_when: You need affordable global VPS or Kubernetes compute with a clean API and more regional coverage than Hetzner, without the cost of AWS/GCP.
avoid_when: You need extensive managed services (databases, ML, serverless), strong EU data sovereignty, or enterprise compliance certifications.

auth.notes: Single API key per account with full access. No fine-grained scoping. Key is created in the Vultr control panel. Always store securely — full account access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Cloud Compute from $2.50/month; High Frequency from $6/month; GPU instances from $90/month
pricing.notes: Very competitive pricing with hourly billing. New accounts often receive $250 free credit for 30 days. No ongoing free tier.

performance.rate_limits: No publicly documented rate limits; practical limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns standard HTTP codes with JSON error messages. Error messages are generally informative but less structured than Hetzner's error codes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Instance creation is not idempotent — same parameters create new instances. Destroy and power operations on correct state are effectively idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API key has full account access — treat as highest-privilege credential, store in secrets manager
Instance creation returns immediately but instance is not ready — poll status until 'active'
Region and plan IDs use short codes (ewr, lax, etc.) — agents must know region slug mapping
Rate limits are not documented but exist — implement exponential backoff on 429 responses
VKE (Kubernetes) clusters can take 5-10 minutes to provision — significantly longer than instance creation

security_score_components.security_notes: API key with IP allowlisting. No granular scope control. SOC2 Type II. Personal firewall and VPC available. DDoS protection included. Simple developer-focused cloud provider.
